_comment: Synthetic git history for the IF-5 fixture. The harness cannot run real git, so this file encodes commits->files (6-month window), plus derived churn / bus-factor / coverage / structural-edge inputs. window_months=6. cochange_support_threshold=3.

commits.author: dana@example.com
commits.message: Raise free-shipping threshold and high-value cutoff for Q1 promo

commits.author: dana@example.com
commits.message: Bump high-value order cutoff to $250 (finance request)

commits.author: dana@example.com
commits.message: Sync high-value threshold after surcharge policy change

commits.author: dana@example.com
commits.message: Adjust thresholds again for spring sale

commits.author: lee@example.com
commits.message: Add createdAt to stored order; thread requiresReview through service

commits.author: morgan@example.com
commits.message: Rename order id prefix and update service callers

commits.author: lee@example.com
commits.message: Add findOrder lookup and getOrder wrapper

commits.author: morgan@example.com
commits.message: Persist status field and surface it from getOrder

commits.author: morgan@example.com
commits.message: Add gizmo SKU and fix price

cochange_pairs.note: Co-change-only. Neither file imports the other. Coupling is via two duplicated magic-number policy thresholds (HIGH_VALUE_ORDER_CENTS / HIGH_VALUE_CENTS = 25000) that are kept in sync by hand. This is the IF-5 hidden-coupling target.
Co-change AND a real import edge. Legitimate, expected coupling — MUST be suppressed by IF-5 (the structural edge explains the temporal coupling).
Below support threshold (1<3) AND has a structural edge — does not qualify on either count.

git_intel._comment: Per-file 6-month git signals reused by IF-5 ranking. churn_6mo = lines changed; commit_count_6mo = commits touching the file; distinct_authors drives bus_factor (1 author => bus_factor=1, single-author hotspot); coverage_pct from the test report; has_tests/has_docs are discovery.md Legacy Health Flags.

history_depth._comment: Full history available => IF-5 certainty is normal (not [LOW]). A shallow/young repo would set thin_history=true and force [LOW] + a stated-coverage note.
